title: Meeting of the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, with British Counterpart, Yvette Cooper
content:
During her visit to London on 9–10 February 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, held a meeting with the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, Yvette Cooper.

The discussions highlighted the excellent level of bilateral dialogue, based on dynamic political, economic, sectoral and security relations, as well as on a solid Strategic Partnership.

The most recent milestones in the bilateral relationship were recalled, including the visit of the President of Romania, Nicușor Dan, to London in December 2025, which included a private audience with His Majesty King Charles III, as well as the holding, in October 2025 in Bucharest, of the first Romanian–British Strategic Dialogue since the signing of the renewed Joint Declaration on the Strategic Partnership between Romania and the United Kingdom (London, 2023).

The two ministers reconfirmed their shared vision on peace and security, as well as their firm commitment to Euro-Atlantic security, reflected also in the United Kingdom’s participation, within NATO, in missions deployed on Romanian territory.

At the same time, interest was expressed in continuing to capitalize on the outstanding contribution made to strengthening bilateral relations by the more than 1.3 million Romanian citizens living in the United Kingdom.

The two officials noted the dynamic trends in bilateral economic exchanges, highlighting the significant potential for cooperation in the economic field and in terms of mutual investments.

At the proposal of Minister Oana Țoiu, the establishment of a working group on trade and investment was agreed, within the framework of the bilateral strategic partnership. This working group will define priority areas and opportunities of common interest, as well as outline an action plan.

According to available data, in the first ten months of 2025, trade in goods amounted to EUR 3.48 billion, with the United Kingdom ranking 11th among foreign direct investors. The initiative of the Working Group is part of the efforts aimed at stimulating strategic investments in areas such as renewable energy, retail, healthcare, technology and IT.

Boosting Romania’s economic cooperation with the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, one of Romania’s most valuable strategic partners, represented a priority for the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, during the London meeting.

An important positive development welcomed during the discussion with the British counterpart was the entry into force of the Convention between Romania and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland for the elimination of double taxation with respect to taxes on income and capital gains. The conclusion of this Convention reflects the desire of both parties to update the legal framework in the field, previously governed by the bilateral Convention on this matter from 1975.

In order to ensure the rapid and transparent application of the new rules, Minister Oana Țoiu signed, on 29 January, the ministerial order for publication in the Official Gazette, thus completing the administrative steps necessary for the benefits of the Convention to be immediately accessible to businesses and citizens.

The new Convention, which has taken effect in Romania as of January 2026, offers numerous advantages. Alignment with OECD standards provides British and Romanian investors with enhanced fiscal stability and predictability, transparent rules and anti-abuse protection—essential conditions for attracting investment and developing economic cooperation. At the same time, it will serve as the basis for cooperation between the competent tax authorities, with a view to eliminating double taxation, exchanging information, providing assistance in tax collection, and combating unfair tax practices in relations between the two states.

“We share the vision of a more competitive Europe, and for us this objective primarily means stronger partnerships with the United Kingdom. Our economic partnership is robust, with trade exchanges exceeding EUR 10 billion and a positive balance for Romania, while our cooperation as allies keeps our skies and seas safer. Today, we agreed to launch, by this summer, the joint Romania–United Kingdom working group on trade and investment, as well as the roadmap for Romania’s accession to the OECD in 2026. Two excellent pieces of news confirm our concrete actions for business-friendly reforms: today we published in the Official Gazette the final step to avoid double taxation between companies in Romania and the United Kingdom—the MAE ministerial order—and, also today, the fiscal policy milestone required for OECD accession was approved. We also discussed our communities, citizen safety, the fight against organized crime, and the cultural richness they bring to both societies. We additionally addressed the issue of English language courses in Romania, the Romanian language lectureship at Oxford, and expanding access to Romanian language courses in British schools,” Oana Țoiu stated.

During the dialogue, Minister Oana Țoiu highlighted Romania’s substantial progress in the OECD accession process, stressing that the country has advanced at an accelerated pace in fulfilling the roadmap, with a focus on the seven critical milestones planned for the first half of 2026. These milestones directly target the business environment, addressing the corporate governance of state-owned enterprises, the digitalization of tax administration, and alignment with international integrity standards in commercial transactions.

The Romanian official also underlined Romania’s support for strengthening cooperation between the European Union and the United Kingdom, based on their shared commitment to international values, principles and rules.

In the context of discussions on European and Euro-Atlantic security, Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized that Romania will prioritize capability development, improved interoperability, the stimulation of defense production, and the harnessing of innovation, while ensuring complementarity between EU and NATO processes. The Romanian side stressed the importance of continuing the excellent cooperation with British partners, both in combating disinformation and in strengthening strategic communication capabilities.

title: Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu positions Romania as a core transatlantic partner in economic security at the Washington rare minerals ministerial hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and US Vice-president J.D Vance
content:
As a strategic partner of the United States and a core EU member country in the rare minerals domain, Romania joined the first rare minerals ministerial at the invitation of Secretary of state Marco Rubio.

The Washington ministerial and the conversations leading up to it had a clear goal to advance economic security, resilient critical mineral supply chains and global partnerships that protect our industries and jobs in the future.

At a time when Romania’s defence, tech and energy industries have a growing need of resources from global supply chains, being part in international negotiations becomes essential.

Romania is aligned with the United States risk assessment. As part of our Strategic Partnership we fully support the idea to create a global coalition to protect industries from political coercion and supply chain disruption by strengthening transatlantic resilient supply chains through market and policy instruments.

As part of the core group of five European ministers of Foreign Affairs at the ministerial, Romanian minister Oana Țoiu highlighted the fact that we can no longer fully separate economic plans from security needs in this geopolitical context. Minister Țoiu advocated for stronger EU-US ties on investments, processing and common market instruments.

“We all had a wake-up call regarding our supply chain dependencies, but it is the US invitation that accelerated the action to de-risk. Romania is a strong supporter of transatlantic ties. Advancing multilateral agreement on rare minerals is the right path. We will use our strong partnerships to further build prosperity and jobs for our citizens. Predictability for our industries and safe sustainable practices become possible only if those that have price dumping practices face a coordinated response.

We have more than 120 years of working with the US to invest together to bring natural resources into our economies, starting with energy and building further opportunities both bilaterally and in the EU - US framework.

Recognized as a country rich in resource, home of some of the largest copper deposits in Europe, with key projects in magnesium, graphite and resources for magnets, we have a national strategy aimed at increasing investments and the processing of raw materials into finite products. Our GDP has already increased 10 times in a generation, but we are aware that any future growth of our key industries (car industry, energy, tech, defence, appliances) depends on Romania being part of resilient supply chains. We are ready to build them fast” minister Țoiu mentioned in her remarks.

“Romania is emerging as a strategic player in Europe’s critical minerals supply chain. Driven by EU-wide efforts to reduce dependence on external sources, the country has taken bold steps to revive key mining operations and expand exploration activities. The Romanian government, in collaboration with the EU, has prioritized strategic investments and licensing mechanisms”, was the evaluation of the International Trade Organization of the United States of America.

In the next 30 days, a final Memorandum of Understanding, with negotiations led by the EU Commission and the State Department, will advance the development of coordinated trade policies and mechanisms, in the context of the European Critical Raw Material ACT.

Romania is ready to contribute for the defence and high-tech industry in terms of magnets, battery and energy storage, aerospace and defence, green energy transition and also sees opportunities for recycling to increase resource efficiency and sustainability and welcome future European stockpiling plans.

Critical minerals and rare earths are essential for our most advanced technologies and will only become more important as AI, robotics, batteries, and autonomous devices transform our economies.

Background information

Romania attended the 2026 Critical Minerals Ministerial in Washington at the personal invitation of Secretary of State Marco Rubio, joined by Vice President JD Vance, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, Energy Secretary Chris Wright, and U.S. Trade Representative Ambassador Jamieson Greer. The ministerial hosted a core group of 5 ministers of foreign affairs from the EU (Romania, Italy, Poland, Netherlands, Lithuania), and ministers and representatives of 54 countries and the European Commission.

title: Meeting of NATO Ministers of Defence
content:
GENERAL

Allied Defence Ministers will meet at NATO Headquarters in Brussels on 12 February 2026. The meeting will be chaired by the NATO Secretary General, Mark Rutte.

Media representatives without annual accreditation to NATO can apply for ad-hoc accreditation to the Ministerial meeting. The deadline for accreditation is Sunday 1 February at 23:59 (CET) .

PROGRAMME

A detailed media programme of the meeting of NATO Defence Ministers will be available on the NATO website closer to the event.

For information about individual Ministers’ media programmes, please contact the national delegations. A list of delegation press officers will be available from NATO Press & Media ( press@hq.nato.int ).

MEDIA ACCREDITATION

Media representatives with an annual accreditation to NATO for 2026 do not need to request accreditation for this event.

All other media representatives wishing to cover the meeting need to apply for accreditation here: link to Accreditation Portal

The deadline to register and apply for media accreditation is Sunday 1 February 2026 at 23:59 (CET) .

Please apply as early as possible to allow for sufficient time for processing accreditation requests.

NATO will confirm accreditation by email. Please bring a printout of the confirmation email when collecting your badge. Media passes must be collected in person upon presentation of the confirmation email, as well as the documents used to apply for accreditation at the Main Entrance, NATO Headquarters, Boulevard Leopold III 1110 Brussels. You must bring the same ID document and the same press card or editor’s letter that you used to apply for accreditation online.

Passes must be visible at all times. Please arrive early to clear security checks. Security personnel will examine and may test equipment and personal effects.

MEDIA ACCESS

Accredited media representatives can work in the press area of NATO Headquarters on the day of the Ministerial meeting, and access all public areas. The opening hours of the press area will be communicated in the media programme.

Accredited journalists can attend Ministers’ doorsteps and the press conferences of the NATO Secretary General. These events will also be streamed live on the NATO website .

National briefings will also take place at NATO Headquarters. Those are by invitation only. Please contact national delegations at NATO for details about their plans.

POOLS

A few elements of the Meeting of NATO Defence Ministers will be accessible only to a pool of visual journalists. Details on pooled events and distribution of pool cards will be available in the media programme, a few days before the event.

If you accept a NATO pool position, you must share immediately all information and material collected while in the pool with any accredited media that requests it, at no charge and with no restriction on the use of the material for news purposes.

Media organisations that want pooled images should first contact the wire service / photo agency of which they are a client. Media representatives and news organisations must identify that it is pooled material every time it is used. Pooled material can only be used for legitimate news purposes and cannot Media be sold.

BROADCAST

NATO will provide broadcast-quality video in real time on EBU World feed or to EBU News Exchange.

NATO photographers and videographers will provide video clips and photos of all public events during the ministerial meeting. Photos can be downloaded from the NATO website . The video files will be available for free download from the NATO Multimedia Portal .

The Secretary General's press conference and public remarks will be streamed live on the NATO website and on NATO YouTube News .

MEDIA FACILITIES

A press working area will be set up on the first floor of the Public Square at NATO HQ. Working spaces cannot be booked in advance and are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Television and radio editing spaces will be available upon request by email to the NATO TV & Radio Unit (see contact details below).

PARKING

Media representatives can park in the Visitors' car park of the NATO HQ. Uplink vans can park in a special section of the car park upon request to the NATO TV & Radio Unit.

For more information:

For general queries: Contact the NATO Press Office

Accreditation NatoAccreditations@hq.nato.int

TV & Radio arrangements on site and via satellite broadcastoperations@hq.nato.int

NATO Video on demand content@natomultimedia.tv

title: Press Release of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs regarding developments in Venezuela
content:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania is closely monitoring the situation in Venezuela, in close coordination with its partners in the European Union.

A community with Romanian roots lives in this country. The first priority for the Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs is the safety of Romanian citizens. Romania’s diplomatic and consular missions in Latin America, as well as the Consular Department of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, are ready to provide assistance if needed, with the support of the EU Delegation in Caracas. A consular coordination mechanism is in place for EU citizens in Venezuela.

Peace in Venezuela is of the highest concern. The Romanian Ministry of Foreign Affairs urges restraint and responsibility on the part of the authorities.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs also urges the initiation of rapid steps for return to democracy, a goal consistently expressed by Venezuelan citizens.

In the spirit of the statement made by the EU High Representative for Foreign Affairs, Kaja Kallas, EU member states are discussing a joint declaration before the convening of a United Nations Security Council meeting on the situation in Venezuela.

We recall that the EU has instituted, with Romania’s agreement, successive sanctions regimes against the undemocratic regime in Caracas, including following the deeply flawed presidential elections in July 2024. The European Union and Romania have not recognized Nicolás Maduro as the legitimate president of Venezuela.

Romania fully shares the priority of combating drug trafficking and organized crime, which represent a significant threat to the security of both continents.

organizations: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Romania

title: Romania becomes OECD Development Assistance Committee’s (DAC) first associate member
content:
Romania has become the first country to obtain the status of associate member of the Development Assistance Committee (DAC) of the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD), the international forum responsible for coordinating official development assistance provided to developing countries.

Romania's accession to the DAC comes in the context of a steady increase in recent years in the volume of official development assistance provided by our country, as well as the strengthening of Romania's profile as a consistent, predictable, and credible donor at the international level. In 2024, the total value of official development assistance provided by Romania reached USD 539.1 million, representing 0.16% of Gross National Income, the highest level recorded by our country so far. This increase was primarily driven by higher contributions at the multilateral level, especially through allocations to the European Union's development budget.

Romania's policy in the field of international development cooperation is based on the development needs of partner countries and on the national expertise and available capacities, with a focus on areas such as institutional development, the green transition, environmental protection, and combating climate change, as well as human development. Official development assistance activities are directed toward ten priority countries in the extended Black Sea region, the Western Balkans, the Middle East, and Africa. The Republic of Moldova represents Romania's top partner, with our country actively supporting the European path of the neighboring state, including within the framework of the Strategic Partnership between Romania and the Republic of Moldova for the European integration of the Republic of Moldova .

Being an Associate Member of the OECD Development Assistance Committee provides Romania with an opportunity to make better use of its comparative advantages and donor experience, while contributing to strengthening and making more effective the national system for delivering official development assistance and increasing the impact of international development cooperation actions for the benefit of partner countries.

The OECD Development Assistance Committee was established in 1961 and originated from the Development Aid Group set up in 1960 within the framework of the Organisation for European Economic Cooperation (OEEC). With the transformation of the OEEC into the OECD, the DAC became a permanent body of the Organization. The Committee brings together the main global providers of official development assistance, aiming to promote cooperation for development and policies that contribute to sustainable development, poverty alleviation, reducing inequalities and improving living standards in developing countries.

Main steps in the process of Romania’s accession to the OECD Development Assistance Committee:

• On July 4, 2024, at the proposal of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Romanian Government approved the initiation of the process of Romania's accession to the OECD Development Assistance Committee.

• On July 5, 2024, the Minister of Foreign Affairs sent a formal letter to the President of the Committee expressing Romania's interest in joining as an associate member.

• On December 19, 2024, Romania sent information to the OECD regarding its compliance with the criteria and conditions for joining the Development Assistance Committee.

• From July 7–10, 2025, the OECD carried out an evaluation mission in Bucharest to assess Romania's compliance with the criteria and conditions for accession to the Development Assistance Committee.

• On November 7, 2025, the OECD Development Assistance Committee decided that Romania met the criteria and conditions for joining the Committee.

• On December 16, 2025, the OECD Council approved the invitation for Romania to join the OECD Development Assistance Committee and sent an invitation letter to Romania's Minister of Foreign Affairs from the OECD Secretary-General.

• On December 17, 2025, Romania obtained the status of associate member of the OECD Development Assistance Committee, following the acceptance of the invitation by Romania's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, through a letter sent to OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann.

The OECD DAC press release is available at: Romania joins the OECD Development Assistance Committee as its first Associate

title: Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session - updated
content:
NATO’s highest Military Authority, the Military Committee, will meet on 21-22 January 2026 in Brussels, at the Chiefs of Defence level.

The Chair of the Military Committee (CMC), Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, will preside the meeting, with the participation of the 32 Allies, along with the Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR), General Alexus G. Grynkewich, and Supreme Allied Commander Transformation (SACT), Admiral Pierre Vandier. Allied Chiefs of Defence will also meet with counterparts from NATO Indo-Pacific Partners, Georgia and Ukraine.

In the first session, SACEUR will brief the Chiefs of Defence on the status of NATO’s readiness to bolster the Alliance’s collective deterrence and defence posture in the face of our common security challenges.

During the second session, SACT will address how NATO can accelerate the delivery of relevant capabilities by supporting Nations in adopting and fielding innovation, at the right scale and pace.

In the NATO-Ukraine Council format, Chiefs of Defence will also discuss the situation on the ground in Ukraine and NATO continued support to Ukraine, including the NATO Security Assistance and Training for Ukraine (NSATU) and the Joint Analysis, Training and Education Centre (JATEC). The Chair of the EU Military Committee (CEUMC) is also invited to participate in this session.

The following sessions will stress the strategic role of Partnerships to address interlinked and evolving global security challenges with counterparts from NATO Indo-Pacific Partners and Georgia.

CMC, SACEUR and SACT will hold a Joint Press Conference upon conclusion of the meeting.

Media opportunity

Wednesday 21 Jan 2026

08:30 (CET) Opening remarks by:

Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, Chair of the NATO Military Committee.

Thursday 22 Jan 2026

13:00  (CET) Joint press conference with:

Admiral Giuseppe Cavo Dragone, Chair of the NATO Military Committee (CMC);

General Alexus G. Grynkewich, Supreme Allied Commander Europe (SACEUR);

Admiral Pierre Vandier, Supreme Allied Commander Transformation (SACT).

Media representatives wishing to attend the press conference are invited to contact the NATO IMS Public Affairs and Strategic Communications (PASCAD) Office via email ( pascad@hq.nato.int ) with a completed accreditation form no later than 1200hrs (Brussels Time) on Monday, 12 January 2026.

Media representatives with permanent accreditation to NATO for 2025/26 are not required to apply for ad-hoc accreditation.

Media accreditation forms

Please download the accreditation form and send it via email with the required attachments : English | French

In case of issues, please email pascad@hq.nat.int

Once approved, Media passes will not be mailed to applicants, but must be collected in person at the Guard House South, NATO Headquarters, Boulevard Leopold III, Brussels, upon presentation of a valid ID card / passport, along with a valid national Press pass (or accreditation letter from a recognized media organisation), and a copy of the confirmation email of successful accreditation.

Passes must be always worn visibly during the stay at NATO Headquarters. Security personnel may ask to check another form of ID, at any time. Media representatives are informed that security personnel will examine and may test equipment and personal effects carried onto the site. Media representatives are also advised to arrive at NATO Headquarters with sufficient lead-time to complete their in-processing.

Livestreaming

The opening remarks delivered by the NATO Chair of the NATO Military Committee will be streamed live on the NATO website and NATO International Military Staff Youtube Channel .

The press conference will also be streamed live on the NATO website and NATO International Military Staff Youtube Channel . The live feed will be provided to EBU.

Video footage will be available for free download from the NATO Multimedia Portal after the event.

Imagery

Following each event, photos, video and audio files will be made available on the NATO IMS webpage www.nato.int/ims , as well as on the Military Committee in Chiefs of Defence Session (MCCS) event page .

Social media

Latest information and photos from the MCCS will be posted on the following X accounts: @CMC_NATO and @NATO_PASCAD .

Please use the hashtags #NATOCHoDs and #NATOMC when posting about the NATO Military Committee.

Media enquiries

Capt (N) Giovanni Galoforo, Public Affairs and Strategic Communications Advisor to the NATO Military Committee and the NATO International Military Staff.

Tel: +32 2 707 5983 E-Mail: Galoforo.Giovanni@hq.nato.int

LTC Balkus Tomas, Deputy Public Affairs and Strategic Communications Advisor to the NATO Military Committee and the NATO International Military Staff.

Tel: +32 2 707 1029 E-Mail: Balkus.Tomas@hq.nato.int

Find more background information about the NATO Military Committee

title: NATO Deputy Secretary General to visit the Netherlands
content:
On 11 and 12 December 2025 , the NATO Deputy Secretary General, Ms Radmila Shekerinska, will visit the Netherlands.

On Thursday, 11 December , Ms Shekerinska will visit the Port of Rotterdam, where she will discuss resilience and industry with Dutch representatives of the transport and energy sectors. While in Rotterdam, she will also meet the City Mayor, Ms Carola Schouten.

In the afternoon, the Deputy Secretary General will travel to The Hague, where she will meet the Prime Minister, Mr Dick Schoof.

On Friday, 12 December , Ms Shekerinska will speak at an event organised by the Institute Clingendael and the Netherlands Atlantic Association. She will take part in a panel discussion on the topic “From The Hague to Ankara: NATO and The Future of Euro-Transatlantic Security”. She will also meet senior officials from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the Ministry of Defence and the Ministry of Justice and Security.

Later that day, the Deputy Secretary General will travel to Delft and visit the Innovative Solutions In Space (ISISPACE) premises.

Pictures will be available on the NATO website .

For more information: For general queries: contact the NATO Press Office Follow us on X: @NATO , @DepSecGenNATO and @NATOPress

title: Romania launches, in Addis Ababa, a training programme on post-conflict reconstruction and combating disinformation in Africa
content:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs, through the Romanian Agency for International Development (RoAid), in partnership with the Institute of Foreign Affairs (IFA) of Ethiopia, launched on Monday, October 20, in Addis Ababa, the training programme “Post-conflict Stabilization and Reconstruction – Best Practices in Building Resilience and Fighting Disinformation” .

The programme, taking place from 20–24 October 2025, brings together experts, diplomats, researchers, and representatives of public institutions and civil society from Ethiopia, other African countries, and Romania, with the aim of sharing best practices and experience in post-conflict reconstruction, crisis management, and combating disinformation.

The opening ceremony was addressed by H.E. Jafar Bedru Geletu, Executive Director of the Institute of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia, H.E. Iulia Pataki, Ambassador of Romania to the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, and Ms. Daniela Dobre, Director General of the Romanian Agency for International Development – RoAid.

In her remarks, Ambassador Iulia Pataki highlighted that this programme represents a concrete Romanian initiative supporting peace and stability in Africa through expertise sharing, professional training, and inter-institutional dialogue. “Romania is firmly committed to contributing to international post-conflict reconstruction efforts and to countering threats generated by disinformation and manipulation, which affect democracies across all regions of the world,” the Ambassador stated.

Throughout the week, participants will engage in lectures and interactive exercises led by Romanian and Ethiopian experts, together with specialists from the Institute of Foreign Affairs of Ethiopia.

The sessions will cover topics such as institutional resilience, combating disinformation, the role of civil society and women in stabilization processes, and cooperation between the European Union, African Union, and international partners in the field of peace and security.

Through this initiative, Romania reaffirms its commitment to supporting sustainable development, peace, and stability in Africa, in line with the EU–Africa Partnership and Romania’s International Development Cooperation Strategy.

Additional information:

The programme is implemented by the Romanian Agency for International Development – RoAid, under the coordination of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, as part of Romania’s development cooperation initiatives. RoAid supports partner countries in strengthening institutional capacities, managing crisis situations, and achieving sustainable development goals.

title: Press release  Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu in the Inauguration Ceremony for the Works Related to the Modernization Project of Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant
content:
On Wednesday, September 3, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu, together with President Nicușor Dan, attended the inauguration ceremony for the civil construction works within the Project for the Modernization of Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant.

On this occasion, the Minister of Foreign Affairs commended the progress made on a strategic project that will contribute to Romania's energy security, job creation and sustainable economic development. The Minister also highlighted Romania's cooperation with like-minded, reliable partners such as Canada, the US, South Korea and Italy in the field of civil nuclear energy.

Modernizing Unit 1 will guarantee another 30 years of clean, safe and reliable energy, as well as continuing more than four decades of cooperation with Canada in the nuclear field. This collaboration has contributed to establish Romania as a center of expertise in nuclear energy.

Upgrading Unit 1 will strengthen Romania's energy production capacity, supporting the provision of reliable, competitively priced energy to domestic consumers. At the same time, it will help our country to achieve its ambition of becoming a regional hub capable of contributing to the energy security of neighboring countries and the wider region.

Additional information

Unit 1 of the Cernavodă Nuclear Power Plant began commercial operation in 1996, with an installed capacity of 700 MWe. The modernization of Unit 1 is a strategic project for Societatea Naţională Nuclearelectrica S.A. (SNN), with the aim of extending the life of the CANDU reactor by 30 years from 2029 onwards.

In December 2024, S.N. Nuclearelectrica, the project leader, awarded an Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) contract for the modernization of Unit 1 at Cernavodă. The consortium of partners consists of Candu Energy Inc., the Canadian Commercial Corporation, Ansaldo Nucleare S.p.A. and Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power Co. Ltd.

The Canadian, South Korean, and Italian ambassadors also attended the inauguration ceremony.

Romania plays an active and consistent role in promoting the use of nuclear energy for peaceful purposes. It is a firm supporter of using nuclear energy to produce electricity safely and sustainably, in accordance with international non-proliferation and nuclear safety standards.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, Received Montenegro's Ambassador to Romania, Danilo Brajović, on the Occasion of the Presentation of His Credentials
content:
On Tuesday, September 2, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu received Montenegro's Ambassador to Romania, Danilo Brajović, for the presentation of copies of his letters of credence.

The Romanian minister commended the dynamic progress of the highest-level bilateral political dialogue, as well as that between the foreign ministries of the two countries. She encouraged maintaining this pace, including as a means of boosting sectoral cooperation and diversifying economic exchanges. The two officials emphasized the essential complementarity between economic diplomacy and political-diplomatic dialogue, and expressed their willingness to support the finalization and signing of bilateral sectoral cooperation instruments.

The Romanian official reaffirmed the full support for Montenegro's European path, commending the ambitious goals of the Government in Podgorica and emphasizing the importance of maintaining the pace and quality of the reforms undertaken. In line with the commitments made, Romania remains open to continuing to offer expertise in support of Montenegro's efforts to strengthen its administrative capacity and implement structural reforms.

The good cooperation within NATO was appreciated, with particular reference to the contributions of the two countries to collective defense and regional security, especially on the Eastern Flank.

At the end of the meeting, Minister Oana Țoiu wished Ambassador Danilo Brajović every success in his work in Romania, assuring him of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' full support in fulfilling his mandate.

title: Romania, a Reliable Partner in a Decade of Changes: Conclusions of the 2025 Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy
content:
Romania's active role in strengthening regional security, accelerating economic diplomacy, and advancing efforts to join the OECD were among the main topics on the agenda of the 2025 edition of the Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy (RADR), which ended on Tuesday, August 26.

The working sessions, which took place at the Palace of Parliament, were attended by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, and were themed "A decade of changes: the impact of Romanian diplomacy". The debates confirmed Romania's priorities of strengthening regional security, accelerating economic diplomacy and advancing efforts to join the OECD.

RADR 2025 provided an opportunity for dialogue with ministers, Romanian and foreign officials, representatives of European and Euro-Atlantic institutions, as well as with the members of economic and academic communities.

The President of the Senate, Mircea Abrudean; the President of the Chamber of Deputies, Sorin Grindeanu; and the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, who represent Romania's central administration, participated in and spoke at the opening session. Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan also engaged in an open and honest dialogue with Romania's ambassadors and consuls general during a special session.

During the plenary working sessions, the following foreign guests addressed the heads of diplomatic missions and consular offices:

This year's edition also featured a video message from Elina Valtonen, the Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Finland, and the participation of Luminița Odobescu, the Presidential Advisor, in the working session dedicated to the future of the European Union.

'You can count on Romania! Romania remains a predictable and supportive partner, focused on achieving concrete results for its citizens and the international community,' stated Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu. She also emphasized that Romanian diplomacy had played a decisive role in turning the challenges of the last decade into opportunities for Romania and the region.

The main messages and outcomes of the 2025 edition were as follows:

Romania - a predictable and reliable partner . The central message of RADR 2025 was that Romania is committed to regional stability, European and transatlantic cooperation, and supporting Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova.

Security and resilience in our neighborhood. Discussions during the session dedicated to the 'new strategic reality' emphasized the importance of strengthening deterrence and defense in the Black Sea region, providing comprehensive support to Ukraine and promoting the European integration of the Republic of Moldova .

Regional coordination and partnerships . Bilateral consultations between Romania and the Republic of Moldova focused on interconnections, critical infrastructure projects and sectoral cooperation, including European affairs.

Economic diplomacy for competitiveness . Participants emphasized the importance of diplomatic missions in promoting investment and exports, connecting Romanian companies to value chains and facilitating the adoption of OECD standards by the economy and administration. The goal of joining the OECD by 2026 was reaffirmed as a working benchmark.

Additional information:

The Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy is the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' main forum for strategic reflection, through which the lines of action for the diplomatic network and Romania's foreign policy priorities are established.

RADR 2025 took place on 25–26 August and featured thematic sessions dedicated to economic diplomacy, the OECD, the new regional strategic reality, the future of the European Union and the prospect of an enlarged, safer and more prosperous Europe. The sessions were moderated by Secretaries of State from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs: Clara Staicu, Ana Tinca and Luca Niculescu.

On the margins of the meeting, bilateral consultations were held between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and her Moldovan counterpart Mihai Popșoi, followed by a joint press conference. ( https://www.mae.ro/node/67258 ).

title: Consultations between Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu and Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova, Mihai Popșoi
content:
On Tuesday, August 26, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu held consultations with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova Mihai Popșoi, who was in Bucharest to participate in the Annual Meeting of Romanian Diplomacy.

Following the meeting, the two officials held a press conference, available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67256 . The transcript of the statement by the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs is available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67257 .

Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu extended her congratulations to her counterpart and the citizens of the Republic of Moldova on the occasion of their Independence Day, which is celebrated annually on August 27. She also expressed her gratitude to Deputy Prime Minister Popșoi for taking part in this year's RADR event, which was held under the title "A decade of change: the impact of Romanian diplomacy."

The agenda included topical issues related to the Republic of Moldova's accession process to the European Union, as well as developments in bilateral projects in important areas of cooperation, such as: energy, infrastructure, border crossing points, education and research. The meeting also addressed ways to strengthen the stability and resilience of the Republic of Moldova.

In light of the significant developments taking place at the regional and international levels that are also important for the European future of the Republic of Moldova, Minister Oana Țoiu commended the efforts made by the authorities in Chișinău to promote the EU accession process. The head of Romanian diplomacy reiterated the firm commitment of the President and Government of Romania to supporting the European integration of the Republic of Moldova. This message was also conveyed by Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan a few days ago during his visit to Chisinau on August 23, 2025.

The two officials discussed the current status and prospects for further progress in the following areas: energy interconnections (through high-voltage lines); land transport infrastructure (including the construction of bridges, the rehabilitation of the Iași-Ungheni railway connection, and its electrification); the strengthening of the Republic of Moldova's energy independence (including by supporting its domestic electricity generation capacity); and the streamlining of traffic through crossing points of common border

At the same time, Minister Oana Țoiu commended the Republic of Moldova's authorities for their efforts to prevent and combat foreign interference attempts in the lead-up to the September 28, 2025 parliamentary elections. In this context, she stressed that Romania will continue to strongly support the Republic of Moldova in strengthening its resilience, which is essential to ensuring the integrity of the upcoming electoral process.

title: Minister Oana Țoiu Engaged in an Open Dialogue with Representatives of the Concordia Employers' Confederation to Discuss Ways of Promoting Romania's Economy
content:
On Tuesday, August 12,2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, met with representatives of the Concordia Employers' Confederation to discuss Romania's priorities in the field of economic diplomacy.

The minister emphasized that, especially now, dialogue with the business community is not only useful, but absolutely necessary. She expressed her appreciation for Concordia's active involvement both domestically and internationally, including its participation in platforms such as Business at OECD (BIAC), which promotes the interests of Romanian entrepreneurs.

Minister Oana Țoiu invited the private sector to propose ways in which economic diplomacy instruments, such as bilateral missions and contacts, thematic events, analyses and specialized materials, could support Romanian companies. She commended the Concordia representatives' proposals, particularly those regarding the reform of the economic diplomacy instruments available to the Romanian state, including the Romanian Agency for Investment and Foreign Trade (ARICE).

Oana Țoiu also mentioned her recent meetings with high-ranking Ukrainian officials she had during her bilateral visit from 7 to 9 August and called on Romanian companies to become involved in the reconstruction process of the neighboring country. She encouraged them to participate in tenders and investments in Ukraine, emphasising the significant opportunities for the development of Romanian businesses.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, Presented an Award to the 500,000th visitor to the Romanian Pavilion at Expo 2025 Osaka
content:
On Tuesday, August 12, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu awarded the 500,000th visitor to the Romanian Pavilion at Expo 2025 Osaka, via a live videoconference from Bucharest. This event marked a milestone, highlighting the popularity and relevance of Romania's message at Expo 2025 Osaka, thus confirming the success of our country's participation in the largest international public diplomacy event of the year. “The Romanian Pavilion is one of the most popular at the Expo, ranking in the Top 10 pavilions. The concert by the George Enescu Philharmonic Orchestra from Bucharest was a significant event, particularly as the Enescu Festival is taking place this autumn. The promotion of young Romanians contributed to the theme of societies of the future. There should be no conflict between tradition and innovation in the way we choose to present Romania.”

On this occasion, the Minister and Ferdinand Nagy, the Commissioner General of the Romanian Pavilion at Expo 2025 Osaka, invited all visitors to the Pavilion to discover Romania and experience for themselves that our country is as beautiful and diverse as the story we are telling at Expo 2025 Osaka. “At the same time, this moment reflects our desire to strengthen the bonds of friendship and cooperation between Romania and Japan, founded on mutual respect, cultural exchange and sustainable partnerships.’’

The 27-year-old Aoi Moriyama, visitor number 500,000, received a symbolic prize from the Romanian Pavilion team, consisting of traditional handicrafts made by Romanian artisans, each piece carrying with it a part of our country's cultural heritage and identity.

Romania's participation in Expo 2025 Osaka marks the 25th time our country has attended a world exhibition.  This time, the theme is "Romania, Land of Tomorrow"" in the "Saving Lives" section. In keeping with this theme, the architectural design of the national pavilion was created by Georgiana Muraru and Anca Bodoloacă, two architecture students, and was selected in a competition organized by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and the „Ion Mincu” University of Architecture and Urbanism.

The Pavilion's daily program features artistic performances by students from the National University of Music in Bucharest and Transylvania University in Brașov.

Students from the University of Bucharest, Faculty of Foreign Languages and Literatures, Department of Japanese, who are Japanese speakers, facilitate communication with the audience and present the 26 musical moments and daily creative workshops.

Romania is also present in Osaka with research and innovation projects such as ELI-NP – the world's most powerful laser with applications in medicine and nuclear physics, the MAVIS artificial heart – a revolutionary solution developed by 38 students from the Faculty of Medical Bioengineering and the Faculty of Medicine, Iași University of Medicine, for patients with severe heart failure, and NerveRepack – Intelligent neural system for bidirectional connection with exoprostheses and exoskeletons, a Horizon research project co-funded by the EU, which developed the Bionic Hand project, coordinated by the National Institute of Microtechnology IMT-Romania.

Additionally, during the summer, Romania is the only country that offers free mineral water to visitors on days of extreme heat, thanks to the support of AUR'A Mineral Water.

In the four months since its opening, the National Pavilion of Romania has organized over 6,840 micro-recitals and creative workshops. The program will continue until the end of Expo 2025, on October 13, 2025, with a recital by the National Chamber Choir 'Madrigal – Marin Constantin,' the 'Radu Stanca' Theater of Sibiu, and the Bucharest National Opera Orchestra.

title: Fourth Trilateral Meeting of Foreign Ministers of Romania, Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova
content:
On August 8, 2025, Oana Țoiu, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs, participated in the fourth trilateral meeting of foreign ministers of Romania, Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova. The meeting took place in Chernivtsi and was also attended by Andrii Sybiha, the Ukrainian Minister of Foreign Affairs, and Mihai Popșoi, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova.

The meeting was also attended online by the foreign ministers of Poland and Lithuania: Radosław Sikorski and Kęstutis Budrys, respectively. Issues related to common threats in the Black Sea and the Baltic Sea were discussed, as these require close cooperation to combat.

The meeting highlighted the strategic importance of continuing and strengthening trilateral coordination in light of recent security developments. The format enabled a practical exchange of views on areas of shared interest to the three countries' economies and societies, with the aim of strengthening cross-border connectivity and advancing the European integration agenda.

During the discussions, Minister Oana Țoiu reiterated Romania's firm commitment to continuing to support Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova. She emphasized the importance of achieving a just, sustainable and inclusive peace in Ukraine, the effects of which will be felt not only in the region, but also at the systemic level, including in the Republic of Moldova: “‘While constant Russian propaganda and disinformation seek to undermine and divide our societies and nations, the trilateral cooperation format between Romania, the Republic of Moldova and Ukraine ensures stronger cross-border connectivity and accelerated European integration for Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova, providing a solid foundation for shared prosperity.’

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs confirmed that the new bridge over the Tisza River will be completed by the end of this year. Together with her counterparts, the minister welcomed the introduction of a new railway service between Kiev, Chișinău and Bucharest. Priorities related to opening new border crossing points and modernizing and expanding existing ones were reviewed.

"Through our active coordination, we contribute to ensuring stability and security in the Black Sea region, setting a standard for our neighborhood relations," the Romanian minister added.

At the same time, the Romanian official pointed out that Ukraine's reconstruction process will require adequate coordination among partners, as well as ensuring complementarity with the European integration process. In this context, she highlighted the importance of trilateral coordination in order to capitalize on investments already made in cross-border connectivity and to use future economic opportunities.

During the trilateral meeting between Romania, the Republic of Moldova and Ukraine, the foreign ministers adopted a joint statement which confirmed the significant progress that had been made so far, while also highlighting the important potential of this coordination format.

“Over the past three years, this framework has gained both increased dynamism and substantial substance, based on our shared commitment to deepening joint cooperation projects, particularly in the field of energy connectivity, for the benefit of our countries," stated Minister Oana Țoiu.

The Romanian official commended Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova for their progress in the European integration process, particularly in view of the challenging circumstances arising from the war of aggression against Ukraine. The minister emphasized the importance of maintaining commitment to the reform agenda, and expressed Romania's willingness to share the lessons learned from its own experience.

title: Meeting between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and Chairman of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine, Ruslan Stefanchuk
content:
During the bilateral visit to Kyiv, Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu had an official meeting with Ruslan Stefanchuk, the Chairman of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine.

During the discussion with the Chairman of the Rada, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasized the particular importance of continuing sustained efforts to implement a comprehensive reform process and to modernize the Ukrainian state. The Minister reiterated Romania's support for Ukraine's European integration process and expressed Romania's willingness to share its own experience.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs underlined the particularly important role of Parliament in all these processes, as well as the role of parliamentary diplomacy in strengthening bilateral relations and supporting Ukraine's European path.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs stressed the importance of the Ukrainian Parliament taking into account the specific situation and needs of persons belonging to national minorities in the process of adopting new legislation. This is a topic on which Romania can share its own experience with the relevant Ukrainian institutions, including through dialogue between Romanian parliamentarians and members of the Rada.

title: Meeting between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and Ukrainian Prime Minister, Yulia Svyrydenko
content:
During her visit to Kyiv, Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, held a meeting with Ukrainian Prime Minister, Yulia Svyrydenko.

The meeting provided an opportunity to convey a strong signal about the mutual interest in peace in the region, European integration, and the development of future joint projects

The discussion focused on the subjects of economic cooperation, including that pertaining to the defense industry, in addition to priorities in the domain of connectivity.

The dialogue revealed a shared interest in the continuation of the practice of joint government meetings, given their usefulness in deepening dialogue in various areas and boosting sectoral cooperation. The significance of interconnectivity was emphasized, including in the context of Ukraine's reconstruction process.

The two senior officials reviewed the progress made on several projects, including the bridge under construction over the Tisa River. In this context, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs highlighted the significant investments Romania has made in cross-border projects since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in February 2022. These investments have had a positive regional impact, including economic benefits.

The meeting revealed a mutual interest in facilitating cooperation between Romanian and Ukrainian companies.

The Ukrainian Prime Minister invited Romania and Romanian companies to take part in Ukraine's reconstruction process and invest in the country.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs reiterated Romania's unwavering support for Ukraine's European path, as well as its commitment to sharing its experience of the pre-accession process. Against this backdrop, the Minister emphasized the importance of continuing the reform processes.

The Minister also stressed the importance of fully respecting the rights of persons belonging to national minorities, emphasizing the need to take their specific situation and needs into account in all relevant legislative and administrative processes, especially with regard to education.

title: Meeting between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and the Ukrainian Minister of Foreign Affairs, Andrii Sybiha
content:
During her official visit to Ukraine, Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu held political and diplomatic consultations with her Ukrainian counterpart Andrii Sybiha on August 7, 2025.

Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu reaffirmed Romania's support for Ukraine's independence, sovereignty, and territorial integrity, praising the heroism of the Ukrainian people and the efforts undertaken by the authorities in Kyiv to achieve a just and lasting peace. She also emphasized the importance of Ukraine's European path, commending the significant progress that has been made so far and encouraging continued efforts towards the credible, predictable and comprehensive implementation of the reform process.

“I visited the Okhmatdyt Children’s Hospital and witnessed the resilience of this people. Despite these horrific circumstances, the doctors and nurses, volunteers, mothers, and family members are all contributing. I was deeply impressed by their courage and determination, even among the youngest patients. Perhaps the most important thing that unites us all is the desire to protect our children," stated the Romanian official at the joint press conference.

Minister Oana Țoiu presented the steps taken by the Romanian authorities to support Ukraine and the Ukrainian people, with a special focus on investments in cross-border infrastructure development. She pointed out that these large-scale projects will also play a fundamental role in Romania's active participation in Ukraine's reconstruction, and regional economic development, as well as in enhancing regional resilience. She emphasized the importance of Ukraine being sovereign, independent and European in order to ensure a secure and prosperous Black Sea region. In this context, she commended the importance that the EU and NATO attach to this region, as well as the special role of riparian countries with similar objectives in implementing concrete projects that will bring the strategic vision for the region to life.

“Since this war of aggression began, we have been firm supporters of peace and our neighbors. Let me be clear: this is as much an act of international solidarity between sovereign nations as it is a way to ensure the safety of Romania. Together, we have transformed these challenging circumstances into an opportunity for structured cooperation. We have invested heavily in our infrastructure and opened new border crossing points. We are building new connections between our economies, societies and cultures.”.

The Romanian and Ukrainian foreign ministers held extensive bilateral political consultations, covering all topics on the bilateral agenda. The two officials engaged in an in-depth exchange of views on security, immediate priorities and the long-term prospects for developing bilateral relations, including upgrading them to a Strategic Partnership for Peace, Democracy and Prosperity.

In this context, the Romanian Minister for Foreign Affairs stressed the fundamental importance of protecting the rights of persons belonging to national minorities, including the right to education, freedom of religion and freedom of association. The minister referred to the important role of these individuals in bringing the two countries closer together and in strengthening a lasting relationship based on shared values and aspirations. Oana Țoiu emphasized the distinctive contributions of ethnic Romanians in Ukraine and Ukrainians in Romania in fostering closer ties between the two countries and ensuring a secure and prosperous European future.

“The Romanian and Ukrainian communities in each other's countries are very important. In this regard, I commend the Ukrainian authorities’ intention to declare 31 August Romanian Language Day.”

The Ukrainian minister expressed his appreciation for Romania's support and presented the current dynamic security situation on the ground, as well as the draft legislation being prepared for the implementation of the reform program.

title: Conversation between Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio
content:
On Thursday evening, Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and US Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed ways to strengthen the Strategic Partnership. The discussion reflected their shared commitment to enhancing the bilateral relationship between Romania and the United States, and covered the main areas of cooperation, the status of joint projects, and plans for the future.

Both officials emphasized the importance of strengthening the Romania–US Strategic Partnership and continuing bilateral cooperation in priority areas such as security, defense, energy and investment in innovation.

The Romanian minister emphasized the excellent cooperation with the United States within NATO and at the bilateral level, as well as the essential contribution of the US military presence in Romania to the security of Europe and the Euro-Atlantic area. The Minister also mentioned the significant potential for co-production in the defense industry, which is starting to be capitalized through concrete projects such as those in the fields of ammunition production and high-performance military radars.

Oana Țoiu, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs, conveyed President Nicușor Dan's appreciation for US President Donald Trump's invitation to pay an official visit to Washington early next year. The Romanian Foreign Minister's visit to New York in September will facilitate continued dialogue in preparation for the Romanian President's visit.

In line with the decisions made at the NATO Summit in The Hague, Romania has taken steps to increase its defense budget, strengthen its national capabilities, improve the support provided to allied troops stationed in the country and contribute to the Alliance's objectives regarding deterrence and defense on the Eastern Flank.

Particular emphasis was placed on the strategic importance of the Black Sea. Romania reaffirmed its commitment to cooperating with its allies and international partners to ensure safe navigation and the exploitation of gas resources in the Black Sea.

Ongoing bilateral projects in the energy sector were highlighted, with a focus on the development of reactors 3 and 4 in Cernavodă, as well as small modular reactor (SMR) technology in Doicești. Romania is thus strengthening its position in terms of both regional energy security and the development of transit routes.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs expressed his confidence that the Trump Administration's internal security objectives will be supported by Romania's participation in the Visa Waiver Program, reaffirming the special importance that the Romanian authorities attach to this issue and their commitment to cooperating closely with the US institutions involved, as well as the good interministerial cooperation and the important contribution of the Prime Minister in pursuing this objective.

At the invitation of the United States through Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu announced Romania's intention to adhere to the Joint Statement on the Security and Resilience of Undersea Cables at the UN meeting in September.

Oana Țoiu commended the signing of the EU-US Trade Agreement, emphasizing its importance for the stability of transatlantic relations and reiterated Romania's support for a balanced and constructive compromise between the two sides in the interests of producers, consumers, and investors in the EU and the US.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, in the Debate on "The Future of the Romanian Cultural Institute"
content:
On Tuesday, July 29, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, participated in a debate on “The 'Future of the Romanian Cultural Institute” at the Palace of the Parliament. The debate was hosted by George Gima, a Member of the Romanian Parliament, and was also attended by the President of the Romanian Cultural Institute, Liviu Jicman, and representatives from the cultural sector and the diplomatic community.

The debate is the first in a series of consultations on the future status of the RCI, seeking to achieve a balance between artistic autonomy and diplomatic efficiency, with the ultimate goal of making Romanian culture into a strategic pillar for strengthening Romania's image and influence in Europe and around the world.

Better coordination between the Romanian Cultural Institute and the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and improved relations with the Ministry of Culture and other institutions, are priorities in both President Nicușor Dan's program and that of the Romanian Government.

'I would like to thank everyone present for openly expressing their views. We have a window of opportunity in front of us for an honest and substantive debate on what we want from our cultural diplomacy and the relationship between the objectives of the diplomatic and cultural sectors and the creative industries. Culture isa prestigious resource for Romania's foreign policy, particularly in light of the challenges we are facing today. I would like to listen to the opinion of all the experts and find the right balance between diplomatic actions and the cultural autonomy of the Institute and its artistic projects. I am convinced that we will identify the best options for strengthening the institution and aligning its objectives", mentioned Minister Oana Țoiu.

'This was the first consultation between the cultural sector and the diplomatic community, which brought the issue of reforming the ICR to the forefront. We want to organize a new debate in August so that we can gather the opinions of as many experts and artists as possible," stated George Gima, who is the vice-chair of the Committee on Culture, Arts, Media and Culture, as and member of the Foreign Policy Committee of the Chamber of Deputies.

MP George Gima also presented a comparative study on cultural institutes in EU Member States and the United Kingdom. The study identifies three key analytical dimensions: the legal status of the institutes, their coordination mechanisms and their sources of funding. The majority of European countries (approximately 39%) have cultural institutes that are subordinate to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs; classic examples include the French Institute and the Cervantes Institute. Other countries (21%) have opted for subordination to the Ministry of Culture (e.g. Greece, Lithuania), while some (18%) maintain independent institutes (e.g. the British Council and the Goethe-Institute).

The full study is available here:

The discussion brought together representatives from the Ministry of Culture, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the fields of cultural management and renowned festivals and personalities from the artistic and cultural sectors. Those present included: e Mircea Cărtărescu, Radu Vancu, Corina Şuteu, Gilda Lazăr, Iulia Popovici, Dan Perjovschi, Matei Vişniec, Constantin Chiriac, Ioana Nicolae, Ştefan Teișanu, Lidia Bodea, Şerban Radu, Cristian Hordilă and Mihai Mitrică.

title: Meeting between Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu and the Austrian Federal Minister for European and International Affairs Beate Meinl-Reisinger
content:
On Friday, July 25, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu met with her Austrian counterpart, Beate Meinl-Reisinger, in the context of the official visit of the Romanian President, Nicușor Dan, to Salzburg in Austria.

The Romanian official emphasized the benefits of revitalizing bilateral relations in terms of both political dialogue and the promotion of concrete projects in strategically relevant areas.

Among other things, Minister Oana Țoiu highlighted the existing potential for economic and investment cooperation, noting that Austria is among the top three foreign investors on the Romanian market.

In this context, she referred to opportunities in the energy and financial-banking sectors.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasized the importance of people-to-people relations between the two countries, particularly thanks to the Romanian community in Austria. The minister pointed out that Romanian citizens are the second-largest foreign community in Austria and make a significant contribution to the host country's economic and social life. At the same time, she expressed her appreciation for the openness shown by the Austrian authorities to issues of priority interest to Romanian workers.

Discussions between the two officials also focused on priority topics on the European agenda, such as the advancement of the EU enlargement process and the next Multiannual Financial Framework.

title: Oana Țoiu, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Made Her First Official Visit to Chișinău, where She Met President Maia Sandu and Other Officials
content:
Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu was received on Tuesday, July 22, by the President of the Republic of Moldova, Maia Sandu, the Speaker of the Parliament in Chisinau, Igor Grosu, and the Prime Minister of the Republic of Moldova, Dorin Recean. She also had consultations with the Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova, Mihai Popșoi, with whom he made press statements which are available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67078 . The transcript of the Romanian Foreign Minister's statement is available here: https://www.mae.ro/node/67079

The agenda included the following topics: Moldova's accession process to the European Union; the advancement of bilateral cooperation projects in areas such as energy, infrastructure, culture, and the Romanian language; the strengthening of Moldova's stability and resilience; the improvement of trade ties between the two countries; and the improvement of border crossing infrastructure.

Minister Oana Țoiu conveyed the firm commitment of President Nicușor Dan, Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, and the entire Romanian government to supporting the Chișinău authorities in the process of European integration, during this crucial period for the Republic of Moldova's future in Europe.

During the discussions held in Chișinău, the Romanian minister emphasized the strength of Romania's relationship with the Republic of Moldova, which is based on a shared language, history, and traditions, as well as a joint commitment to Moldova's integration into the European Union. The progress on energy interconnections (such as the Suceava - Balti or Vulcanesti - Chisinau lines), the joint investments in Moldova's energy independence, transport and infrastructure, as well as the topic of traffic fluidization at the shared border, were discussed.

These investments generate tangible benefits for Romanian and Moldovan citizens and businesses. In this context, Minister Oana Țoiu met with representatives of Romanian companies operating in the Republic of Moldova. The regional security situation and joint efforts to combat hybrid attacks were also discussed.

title: NATO and the EU strongly condemn the malicious cyber activities carried out against multiple NATO and EU Member States by entities subordinated to Russia’s military intelligence service (GRU)
content:
NATO and the EU strongly condemn the malicious cyber activities carried out against multiple NATO and EU Member States by entities subordinated to Russia’s military intelligence service (GRU). GRU-subordinated actors have targeted public institutions, critical infrastructure operators and other entities within the Alliance’s and EU’s territory, including in Romania.

These malicious cyber activities, which have intensified on the backdrop of Russia’s war of aggression against Ukraine, are part of an array of more complex hybrid campaigns waged by the Russian Federation against EU Member States and NATO Allies, including Romania, as well as against partners of the two organisations. The aim of these campaigns is both to undermine the stability and democratic resilience of the targeted countries and to deter continued support to Ukraine.

Romania condemns these malicious cyber activities and shares NATO and the EU’s evaluation that they demonstrate the Russian Federation’s disregard of the normative framework on responsible state behaviour in the cyberspace, such as it has been defined by the UN. We call on the Russian Federation to respect its international obligations.

Romania stands in solidarity with other Allies, EU Member States and partners affected by these malicious cyber activities and will continue to engage actively in the intensifying efforts to prevent, deter and respond to such destabilising actions.

Background information :

The North Atlantic Council published, on the 18 th of July, a Statement of condemnation concerning Russian malicious cyber activities, available at:

https://www.nato.int/cps/en/natohq/official_texts_237067.htm

On the same day, the EU High Representative put forward a Statement on behalf of the EU condemning Russia’s persistent hybrid campaigns against the EU, its Member States and partners, available at:

https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2025/07/18/hybrid-threats-russia-statement-by-the-high-representative-on-behalf-of-the-eu-condemning-russia-s-persistent-hybrid-campaigns-against-the-eu-its-member-states-and-partners/

The GRU possesses a very advanced cyber programme, having proven in time that it has the necessary capabilities to support the Russian Federation’s strategic objectives. In this regard, multiple GRU military units are conducting malicious cyber campaigns at international level and are coordinating the activity of APT28/ Fancy Bear , APT44/ Sandworm and APT Ember Bear . These actors’ cyber operations are aimed at governmental institutions, military organisations, critical infrastructure, media publications, NGOs, etc. from Allied countries, EU member states and partner countries.

At the 2021 Brussels NATO Summit, Allies approved the new Comprehensive Cyber Defence Policy. Allies reaffirmed NATO’s defensive mandate and pledged to employ all capabilities in order to deter, defend against and counter the full spectrum of cyber threats during peacetime, crisis and conflict.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu in the Meeting of EU Foreign Affairs Ministers
content:
Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu participated on Tuesday, July 15, 2025, in the meeting of the Ministers of Foreign Affairs of the EU Member States (Foreign Affairs Council/FAC), which took place in Brussels.

The ministers discussed Russia's aggression against Ukraine, the situation in the Middle East, developments in Georgia, the Pact, and the reform of the Union for the Mediterranean. Current affairs covered EU-CELAC relations and the situation in Somalia. High Representative Kaja Kallas also provided a briefing on the EU-China Strategic Dialogue and the EU-China and EU-Japan Summits.

Regarding Russia's aggression against Ukraine, Ukrainian Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha had a video-conference intervention in which he outlined the latest developments on the ground and the discussions on peace in Ukraine, and foreign ministers exchanged views on the EU's multidimensional support for Ukraine.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, has reaffirmed the need for the EU to continue to support Ukraine, focusing on military and financial support, as well as assistance in preparation for the upcoming cold season.

She also emphasized the need to increase pressure on Russia by introducing further restrictive measures. She commended the adoption of an additional five listings under the regime of sanctions for human rights violations in Russia, as well as an additional 15 listings under the sanction regime for Russia's destabilizing actions abroad. These measures are designed primarily to limit Russia's ability to finance the war, as well as to impose economic and political costs on the Russian political figures responsible for human rights violations and destabilizing actions. She expressed support for the swift adoption of the 18th sanctions package and for agreeing an EU-wide formula to limit the movement of Russian diplomats within the Union.

Minister Oana Țoiu expressed the support for advancing Ukraine's and Moldova's accession to the EU and advocated intensifying outreach efforts to global partners with regard to a just and lasting peace in Ukraine, including in the perspective of the upcoming High-Level Week at the UN General Assembly in September 2025.

During the discussion on developments in the Middle East, the situation in Gaza was covered. In this context, Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized the importance of taking measures to improve the serious humanitarian situation. In this regard, she commended the recent constructive dialogue between the EU and Israel, following which, Israel agreed to take significant measures to improve the humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip, to be implemented in the coming days. The Minister also reiterated its support for a peace agreement and the release of hostages.

Regarding Iran, the head of Romanian diplomacy expressed the deep concern about Iran's actions in the region and internationally and disapproval of the Tehran regime's decision to cease cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). The Romanian minister also emphasized the importance of closely monitoring developments in the Iranian nuclear program following the US's partial dismantlement of its main production facilities, as this continues to pose significant proliferation risks and threaten global security.

Referring to the developments in Georgia, the head of Romanian diplomacy expressed concern about the democratic regression of the authorities, as well as the measures restricting freedom of expression and suppressing opposition voices. She also advocated maintaining a principled EU approach to the situation in Georgia, adopting a proportional, reversible and pragmatic reaction while keeping the option of dialogue open, in view of the EU's long-term strategic objective for the region.

Regarding the Pact and the reform of the Union for the Mediterranean, the Romanian official emphasized the importance of supporting the transition from cooperation to a sustainable partnership, and stressed that partners must take responsibility for their actions in order to align EU policies with the specific needs of each state.

On the margins of the FAC meeting, High Representative Kaja Kallas hosted an informal breakfast attended by the Deputy Prime Minister / Foreign Minister of the Republic of Moldova, Mihai Popșoi. The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs pointed out the complex situation in Moldova in the context of Russia's intensified hybrid actions against Chisinau. She commended the adoption, at Romania's initiative, of ten new listings under the EU's restrictive measures regime in view of the destabilizing actions against the Republic of Moldova, as well as two new listings under the restrictive measure regime in view of Russia's destabilizing actions abroad; the persons and entities concerned will be subject to travel bans and asset freezes. Oana Țoiu also underlined the importance of continued support from the EU and Member States and reiterated her support for advancing the EU accession process.

At the same time, in the context of participating in the FAC meeting, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs held meetings with the Spanish Minister for Foreign Affairs, the European Union and Cooperation, José Manuel Albares Bueno, and her Polish counterpart, Radosław Sikorski. The discussions focused on concrete ways to capitalize on the bilateral Strategic Partnerships, with an emphasis on diversifying economic exchanges and regional security, including defense industry cooperation. During the dialog with the Polish official, Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized the interest in working together to use the common opportunities offered by EU funding to participate in the reconstruction of Ukraine. During the meeting with her Spanish counterpart, the Romanian dignitary expressed her confidence in their joint commitment to finalizing the bilateral agreement on dual citizenship.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, Welcomed Angela Ganninger, Ambassador of the Federal Republic of Germany to Romania
content:
