Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu received on Tuesday, July 8, 2025, the Ambassador of the Federal Republic of Germany to Romania, Angela Ganninger, for the presentation of copies of letters of accreditation.

The discussion revealed the excellent level of the strategic relationship between Romania and the Federal Republic of Germany, which takes place this year in the context of the 145th anniversary of the establishment of Romanian-German diplomatic relations.

Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized the Romanian Government's priority to strengthening relations with Germany. The two officials reviewed the topical issues on the bilateral agenda, highlighting the opportunities to deepen the Romanian-German dialogue and cooperation and to coordinate positions in the coming period, in the current international context marked by multiple challenges.

The Romanian minister expressed interest in strengthening bilateral cooperation in economic and sectoral areas, given that Germany is Romania's most important trading partner and German investment and expertise are a key contribution to our country's development.

At the same time, she recognized the special value of people-to-people relations between the two countries, facilitated by the German minority in Romania and the sizeable Romanian community in Germany, which represents a solid bridge between the two countries.

The discussion also covered European priorities aimed at strengthening European security. The Romanian minister expressed Romania's support for strengthening the multidimensional support to Ukraine, as well as for continuing to provide consistent European support to Moldova, both on the path towards EU accession and for strengthening Chisinau's resilience in the face of current threats.

Minister Oana Țoiu wished the German diplomat every success in her mission, assuring her of the Romanian authorities' full support in strengthening the bilateral relationship between Romania and Germany.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, Welcomed the Serbian Ambassador to Romania, Stefan Tomašević, on His Farewell Visit
content:
On Thursday, July 3, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, received the Ambassador of the Republic of Serbia to Romania, Stefan Tomašević, on his farewell visit.

The Romanian Minister expressed her appreciation for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' positive working relationship with Ambassador Tomašević during his term of office in Bucharest, which contributed to the intensification of political and diplomatic dialogue, as well as economic and sectoral cooperation, including at the cross-border level.

At the same time, she stressed the importance of maintaining a consistent pace in this regard, including the implementation of significant connectivity projects for both sides, such as the Timisoara-Belgrade motorway, the Arad-Mokrin gas pipeline, and the resumption of passenger rail connections.

The Romanian minister emphasized the importance that the Romanian minority in Serbia and the Serb minority in Romania have in the development of bilateral cooperation. In this context, he underlined the importance of respecting the rights of persons belonging to national minorities, including the preservation of their cultural identity.

The Romanian official reconfirmed the support for the European course of the Republic of Serbia, emphasizing the Romanian Government's commitment to actively contribute to the EU's enlargement beyond Romania's borders. In this regard, Romania stands ready to contribute its expertise to support Serbia's efforts in implementing reforms aligned with the European agenda.

At the end of the meeting, Minister Oana Țoiu wished Ambassador Stefan Tomašević success in his future work.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, at the Launch Event in Bucharest of the Danish Presidency of the Council of the European Union
content:
Romania's Minister of Foreign Affairs, Oana Țoiu, took part on Tuesday, July 1, 2025, in the launch event in Bucharest of the Danish Presidency of the Council of the European Union, which will take office during the second semester of this year. The meeting was attended by the Ambassador of Denmark to Romania, Uffe Balslev, heads of mission and representatives of the diplomatic corps accredited in Bucharest, as well as representatives of the press.

During the event, the Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasized Romania's support for a successful Danish Presidency, highlighting that it is in the interest of all Member States and the European institutions that this mandate contributes to a more united, secure and resilient Europe in a challenging European and global context.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs expressed Romania's confidence in a solid agenda of priorities, responding to the main challenges facing the EU and preparing European action for the future. Referring to the motto of the new Danish Presidency of the Council of the EU, the Minister of Foreign Affairs pointed out that the response to a changing global context is also an opportunity to change Europe for the better, closer to its citizens. The Minister also emphasized the necessity for ongoing joint efforts in this regard.

She expressed Romania's support for the priorities at European level aimed at strengthening European security, the European democratic model based on respect for the rule of law and an inclusive economic and social construction. In this context, she emphasized the Danish Presidency's support for the objective of a more secure Europe and underlined the importance of an effective European response to hybrid threats. The Minister also stressed the importance of a strengthened European action in the field of defense, in complementarity with NATO and with the maintenance of a solid transatlantic relationship in the current international context.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs also referred to the importance of sustained support for Ukraine and commended the Danish Presidency's focus on this objective, including the European course, the post-conflict perspective and Ukraine's reconstruction.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs commended the Danish vision of dynamizing the enlargement process and mentioned the clear commitment to this dimension reflected in the Government Program, with the objective of an enlarged Europe, going beyond Romania's borders, in less than a decade. She emphasized that the Danish Presidency will have in Romania a partner fully aware of the geopolitical necessity of advancing EU enlargement, both from the perspective of the accession of the Eastern partners, the Republic of Moldova and Ukraine, and of the Western Balkans, on the basis of its own merits. She also underlined the importance of maintaining the European Union's multidimensional support for the Republic of Moldova.

At the same time, Minister Oana Țoiu emphasized Romania's particular interest in advancing European efforts at regional level to strengthen security in the Black Sea area. In this regard, she gave assurances of Romania's active and consistent involvement in the implementation of the priorities defined by the new European Strategy on the Black Sea.

With regard to the EU competitiveness pillar, which is included in the program of the Danish Presidency of the Council of the EU, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasized the importance of strengthening the Single Market, energy efficiency and energy security in an inclusive and accessible manner for all citizens. She also highlighted the importance of convergence at European level, supported by an ambitious European multiannual budget.

The following is the English transcript of the speech delivered by the Minister of Foreign Affairs

***

Thank you, Ambassador Balslev. You are perfectly right that the challenges are clear, but the opportunities lie within the challenges themselves and I am grateful to be here today with you, I am grateful to you for hosting us today. I also want to welcome the presence of Ms. Ramona Chiriac, Head of the European Commission Representation, it is always a joy to be together at events.

It is a good moment, also, obviously, to thank the previous Polish Presidency of the Council for all its work. We have had this conversation with the Polish delegation in The Hague, as well, and we have conveyed our thank you and the respect we have towards the dedication towards our common agenda in the first Semester. This was by no means an easy task. But is it ever?

I am certain Denmark, with your strong energy and commitment in steering the Council's work for the next six months will be a great ally for Romania continuing forward.  I believe it is fair to say that this mandate takes place at a pivotal moment for the Union, both internally and in terms of its external action.

You have named it A Strong Europe in a Changing World , but for the following months we might also consider the fact that it's also A changing Europe in a changing world . And I think that is for the best. I am confident in your vision and in your consensus-building skills, as this is your eighth time holding the Council Presidency and I think it is the right time to have someone with such strong experience in leading our common agenda. I wish you every success during your tenure at the helm of the EU Council.

This Semester will definitely be an intense one. We have a joint duty to advance our common European project that remains our solid answer to a challenging regional and global environment, marked by uncertainty, strategic and economic competition and rising levels of volatility and conflict.

It is clear though that we have to work on multiple levels. And here I have seen that Denmark is addressing this very clearly. And there is no competition between the agendas and between the areas. It is quite clear that to have a safe Europe, to have a Europe that is better at defending itself, we need a strong market. And we need not just to preserve the single market, but to make sure we create there, as well, the changes that we need. That the green transition happens, but that it happens in a fair way. In a fair way that is seen and perceived as inclusive, not just by small businesses, but also by citizens that don't feel, let's say, at the distance to this transition. We need to continue the work on improving the instruments of our security and defense, yes, in cooperation and in complementarity with NATO but you have said it very clearly that as a European Union we need to be more resilient, we need to be less dependent, we need to stay true to our democratic values enshrined in the treaties, to human rights and respect for the rule of law, as well as our unique and inclusive economic and social model. And let us be clear, our economic and social model will also be challenged.

We have taken a strong, bold decision to increase our budget and to increase our defence allocation, but it was quite clear also in the conversation I had with the other ministers of Foreign Affairs in The Hague and at the EU Council, that it's quite clear to us that although currently in most countries and especially in Romania citizens have a clear support for larger NATO budgets, we need to have this conversation day in and day out to make sure that they understand that peace and security are also in the interest of a growing economy, are also in the interest of the social inclusion model, and that they cannot be separated. Because it is, at the end of the day, their money from their taxes, their work and their contribution. And it is to be clear that defense spending and investments in those areas are felt also in the other areas where citizens are highly interested in and they don't see it as a competition on budget and their taxes.

We need to protect our democratic societies, as you have clearly said, from new hybrid threats and external interferences into our democratic processes. And here we believe the recent experience in Romania helps us be a voice and a contributor to make sure that when this is happening in other countries in the region, potentially, we are there to help, we are there to share our experiences, to share the lessons we have learned. And to share the gratitude we have towards our citizens, because at the end of the day, the European values, the European friendship is what has made a lot of our citizens step up and protect the democratic model and the European path forward. And we believe with this common experience as societies, within our institutions, within the way media has made sure it showcased clearly what was happening, that we can be of help to our friends in the region and we can be a relevant voice within the European Union in this direction and within NATO.

I cannot underline enough how important it is if we are to build a stronger and more resilient Union to advance enlargement, a key strategic policy for the future of our continent. This is why we need to step up our efforts in order to receive new members in the European Union family in a few years' time. Those that are performing should join us as soon as possible. Not in a distant future, but in the near future.

As some of you might have noticed in the Government Programme that we have passed through Parliament less than 10 days ago, one of the strategic objectives that we have put there for the Government, for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, was that in less than a decade we want to support the idea that the European Union border doesn't stop with the Romanian border anymore. And this is something that has currently the support of the Parliament through the vote for the new Government and the new Government Programme. And it is something we have been clear about and we want to be even a stronger advocate of that.

There is no doubt that the complexity of the current international situation requires our strong and united European action. It is exactly as the Danish priorities programme entitled A Strong Europe in a Changing World clearly states several times throughout. I am also convinced that our strength lies in our unity and our capacity to act together. I am looking forward to working with you to fulfil this mission. It has also been the key message of our Romanian President Mr. Nicușor Dan, conveyed through the European Council. And we think Romania has a strong legitimacy because we have always been a strong ally and a strong friend for the European Union, within the European Union and for its enlargement. You can fully count on Romania to continue being as active and as a reliable partner as it has always been.

Your Excellency, I cannot but share the strong focus that the current Presidency is placing on a secure Europe, starting from the basic premise that the European Union is to become a stronger geopolitical actor, both in its strategic neighbourhood and on the world stage.

We have been moving closer toward this strategic goal in the past years, but now it is time to act more decisively. Clearly, our collective action is to tackle a wide array of security challenges from the many and interconnected strands of internal and external security to building resilience against conventional and hybrid threats. And with the hybrid threats I think that's where we have a higher challenge because we do not always have institutions that have the proper definitions, that have had the proper preparation and I think in recent years this has advanced and it can be the key for this year as well. Consolidating of global partnerships, chief among remaining, in my opinion, a robust transatlantic relationship. Although clearly a changing one, currently. Transatlantic cohesion is all the more important in the current complex security environment.

We fully share the view that the EU must be able to defend itself by taking responsibility for its own and Euro-Atlantic security, working closely and in complementarity with NATO. We also need to become a more competitive and greener Europe, the second pillar of the Danish Presidency programme, and we are satisfied that this practical necessity is clearly captured in the programme. Especially as Romania, as you have so gracefully mentioned, has contributed significantly through time for this agenda. We plead for a balanced and tailor-made approach meant to strengthen long-term competitiveness while responsibly advancing the green transition. We have to make sure that the green transition, just like the digital one, leaves no one behind. Competitiveness and convergence depend on each other and should be achieved in parallel. A strengthened Single Market and simplified rules will go a long way towards boosting our competitiveness and unlocking additional investment capacity to the benefits of citizens, businesses and national authorities.

One particular point related to the broader discussion of competitiveness refers to energy. This is key for Romania and this is key for the region as well. So we welcome the focus placed by the Danish Presidency on driving Europe towards ensuring a sufficient production of clean, affordable energy. At the same time, energy security and affordability remain crucial pillars of our common approach, as high energy prices continue to be an issue affecting industrial competitiveness and affordability. At the same, our ambition to create a more competitive and greener Europe should be backed by an ambitious Multi-Annual Financial Framework. And we look forward to being engaged in this negotiation. Those that are poised to be complicated talks, as always, but I'm sure that the Danish Presidency will play a constructive role by acting as an honest broker, as it has always done.

Ladies and gentlemen, diving deeper into some of the various dimensions of a secure Europe underlined by the Danish Presidency, I would like to touch shortly on several additional aspects. First, on Ukraine. Let me underline once more that it is very important to continue and reinforce our support for the country, and for the Ukrainian population. This was one of the very recent conclusions, and this is a conclusion that Romania supports. We aim for a complete and unconditional ceasefire, and that remains essential towards a lasting peace. We need to keep European and international aid flowing to Ukraine. As such, we welcome the Presidency's commitment to ensure that the EU maintains its leading role in providing predictable and coordinated support to Ukraine, including as part of its accession path. We are also looking forward towards the post-conflict reconstruction phase, and Romania are also wants to take a more active role in those discussions.

At the same time, we as Europeans must continue to put pressure on Russia to seriously engage in peace talks. In this regard, the swift adoption of the 18th package of sanctions targeting further energy and financing is key. We welcome the recent agreement of EUCO to prolong the already implemented sectoral measures.

Last but not least, a more secure Europe means a more secure Black Sea region. In this respect, we welcome the European strategic approach in the Black Sea, as reflected in the most recent European Council conclusions, something that Romania has actively contributed to. Raising EU's level of ambition for the Black Sea demonstrates the EU's commitment to adapting its posture to new geopolitical realities. We trust that this approach will contribute to the restoration of peace and security, the economic development in the region.

Your Excellency, the last point that I want to point out, as I clearly stated, the key priority for Romania on the European agenda remains advancing the enlargement, as you have described it correctly, a geopolitical necessity. We remain strong supporters of the EU enlargement towards both the Eastern partners, Ukraine and the Republic of Moldova, and those of the Western Balkans, on a merit-based approach. All countries have made significant efforts with impressive results under most difficult circumstances at time. We need to move under the Danish Council Presidency to be able to open negotiation clusters, starting with the first one, Fundamentals. Continuing EU steadfast support and multifaceted support for the Republic of Moldova in political, diplomatic and financial areas is essential for the stability, democratic path and EU integration course of the Republic of Мoldova. Advancing on the EU track would send a strong message of European support in view of the upcoming elections as well.

Looking forward to the EU - Republic of Moldova Summit to be held in Chisinau in a couple of days.

Advancing our concrete work within the European Union driven by a logic of unity and solidarity will be the most effective political message that the Union can send in this difficult geostrategic context. I assure you that Romania will continue to engage constructively in the EU and in all related debates, aimed at finding the best solution for existing and future challenges.

On this positive note, let me conclude by wishing, again, all the best of luck to our Danish friends in pursuing their mandate for the benefit of our common European project.

Thank you.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu in the Summit and Ministerial Meeting of the South-East European Cooperation Process
content:
On Monday, June 16, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu participated in the Summit and meeting of foreign ministers of the South-East European Cooperation Process (SEECP) in Tirana.

The meetings marked the end of Tirana's term as chair and were led by the Albanian President, Bajram Begaj, and the Albanian Minister for Foreign Affairs, Igli Hasani.

In his remarks, Minister Hurezeanu reiterated Romania's full support for accelerating the EU's enlargement policy based on merit in the reform process. This support should be given equally to both the Western Balkans and the Eastern Neighborhood, particularly the Republic of Moldova and Ukraine.

He referred to the benefits of implementing the necessary reforms for European integration, such as ensuring sustainable economic growth. These reforms will also contribute to the integration of the advantages arising from current transitions in digitalization and energy, while strengthening the rule of law and commitment to democratic values.

In this context, the Romanian official emphasized the importance of EU candidate countries aligning themselves with the common foreign and security policy as a clear indicator of their geostrategic orientation. He also encouraged the use of EU support instruments dedicated to the region, such as the Growth Plans for the Western Balkans and the Republic of Moldova, and the Ukraine Facility.

The Romanian official also referred to the significant geopolitical challenges that persist in the region as Russia continues its war of aggression against Ukraine. He reaffirmed Romania's commitment to supporting Ukraine and Moldova in this challenging context. He confirmed that Romania would remain actively involved in regional cooperation initiatives, including those related to security

At the end of the Summit, Sofia took over the SEECP Chairmanship-in-Office for the next term.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu in the Ministerial Council Meeting of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (MCM OECD 2025)
content:
On Tuesday, 3 June, Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu attended the annual ministerial-level meeting of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) at the Organisation's headquarters in Paris.

The meeting was chaired by Costa Rica, with Australia, Canada and Lithuania serving as vice-chairs. The event brought together high-level representatives from the 38 OECD member countries, candidate countries and partner countries, as well as from international organisations.

This year's edition, which was themed 'Leading the way towards resilient, inclusive and sustainable prosperity through rules-based trade, investment and innovation', provided an opportunity to share best practice and identify ways to enhance resilience and prosperity in the context of challenges to international trade. The ministerial discussions covered the impact of economic, climate-related and social risks, emerging challenges, and the capitalisation of the digital economy.

During the session dedicated to promoting inclusive economic growth to achieve sustainable development goals, the head of Romanian diplomacy delivered a speech. On this occasion, ministers discussed public policy initiatives to support the green transition and the blue economy through rules-based trade, promoting an inclusive approach and the participation of SMEs.

In his speech, the Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasised Romania's contribution to the OECD's sustainable development efforts and national initiatives within the broader context of the commitment to joining the OECD. During his speech, the Minister mentioned the effective alignment of investments and financing with sustainability objectives, as well as the promotion of partnerships and regional cooperation as ways to implement the 2030 Agenda. In terms of Romania's contributions, the minister highlighted the establishment of the Investment and Development Bank as a means of strengthening investments in line with the Sustainable Development Goals, the issuance of green bonds by Romania to finance sustainable projects, and its participation in Black Sea cooperation projects with partners in the field of the blue economy.

On the margins of the event, Minister Hurezeanu met with OECD Secretary-General Mathias Cormann. During the meeting, he reviewed the status of the accession process and reiterated Romania's commitment to successfully completing it by 2026, in line with the Organisation's policies and practices. The Romanian Minister commended Secretary-General Cormann's efforts to promote the Organisation's enlargement and thanked him for his support throughout the accession process.

Additional information

The OECD Ministerial Council Meeting is the most important annual event hosted by the Organisation. It is attended by ministers of foreign affairs, the economy, finance and trade, as well as other government representatives from member countries, candidate countries and OECD partner countries, along with representatives from international organisations. The meeting provides ministers with an opportunity to discuss global socio-economic challenges and the essential role played by the Organisation as a forum for dialogue in identifying common responses based on objective data.

This year's session is the fourth ministerial-level meeting of the OECD Council in which Romania has participated as a candidate country since the opening of accession discussions in 2022.

Further information on the 2025 edition of the MCM, as well as previous editions, can be found at: https://www.oecd-events.org/meeting-of-the-council-at-ministerial-level-2025/en/ .

title: Message from Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu on the occasion of the Day of Romanians Everywhere
content:
Romanians Everywhere Day is a symbolic occasion for celebrating Romanians living abroad, including those who are part of historical communities located near the borders, as well as those who are part of the Romanian diaspora. This day reminds us of the fundamental values that unite us and that we believe in, as well as our shared linguistic, spiritual and cultural heritage. It also serves as a reminder of the sacrifices made by those who fought for freedom and a prosperous, developed society.

Today, we reflect on the winding historical journey and the circumstances (often difficult) that have kept us apart. At the same time, we appreciate the consolidation of a national diaspora undergoing historic expansion across the globe. This diaspora has found its distinct voice within the countries in which it lives, while continuing to play an active role in the life and development of Romanian society.

The international context and technological advances have made distances relative. Now more than ever, the diaspora is closer to home, wherever they may be. At the same time, the diaspora plays an integral role in shaping Romania's international reputation. Following Romania's accession to the North Atlantic Alliance and the European Union, the profile of Romanians abroad has developed in tandem with our society's progress towards prosperity and security. The diaspora contributes significantly to this progress.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs continues to promote and defend the rights of Romanian citizens abroad through Romanian diplomatic missions and consulates abroad. These provide consular services, protection and assistance in normal circumstances and in emergencies. They also work to strengthen ties with the country and promote Romanian identity, values, and historical and cultural heritage.

In a global context marked by numerous security challenges and instability, we are stronger and better prepared to face threats when we stand together, united in our determination to build a better future for everyone.

Happy birthday to all Romanians, wherever you may be!

title: Consultations between Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu and Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova, Mihai Popșoi
content:
On Friday, May 23, 2025, Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu held consultations with Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Moldova Mihai Popșoi, who was in Bucharest to participate in a series of international conferences on current regional issues.

During the discussions, the two officials reaffirmed the close and special relationship between Romania and the Republic of Moldova, addressing current aspects of bilateral relations with a focus on ongoing joint projects and Romania's support for the Republic of Moldova's European integration process.

Minister Emil Hurezeanu reiterated the interest of Romania's president-elect, Nicușor Dan, in visiting Chișinău as one of his first foreign engagements. He also confirmed the continued and steadfast support of the Romanian government in deepening political dialogue and concrete cooperation projects with the Republic of Moldova.

The two officials also reviewed the main current developments in the regional context, which is dominated by security threats and hostile hybrid actions. In this context, Minister Emil Hurezeanu confirmed that Romania will continue to support the Republic of Moldova in its process of reform, strengthening its resilience and preparing for accession to the European Union.

At the same time, issues relating to the Republic of Moldova's internal political developments were discussed, particularly in view of the parliamentary elections scheduled for September 28, 2025, and the process of resolving the Transnistrian issue.

The Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs thanked the authorities in Chișinău for their support in ensuring the smooth running of the presidential elections at the 64 polling stations organized by Romania's diplomatic missions in the Republic of Moldova.

title: Participation of Minister of Foreign Affairs, Emil Hurezeanu, in the Timișoara Award for European Values award ceremony for the President of the European Parliament, Roberta Metsola
content:
On Thursday, May 22, 2025, Minister for Foreign Affairs, Emil Hurezeanu, attended the Timișoara Award for European Values ceremony in Timișoara, where the award was presented to the President of the European Parliament, Roberta Metsola. Now in its second year, the Timișoara Award for European Values honors personalities recognized internationally for promoting European values.

In his speech delivered during the ceremony, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Emil Hurezeanu, emphasized the symbolic importance of presenting this award in Timișoara, a city that symbolizes the December 1989 Revolution, at a time when Romania is reaffirming its commitment to the European project.

Minister Emil Hurezeanu expressed his gratitude to President Roberta Metsola for her commitment to promoting an ambitious European project and for her excellent collaboration with Romania throughout her time in the European Parliament, particularly in her role as President of the European legislative forum.

The Minister of Foreign Affairs commended President Roberta Metsola for her consistent support for Romania's accession to the Schengen area, for her personal support for the European path of the Republic of Moldova. He also praised her active involvement in the process of reforming, streamlining, and increasing the transparency of the European Parliament.

At the same time, the Minister of Foreign Affairs highlighted Roberta Metsola's valuable contribution to upholding European values and promoting the European project among European citizens, especially young people. In this context, he mentioned her meetings with young people in Romania.

On the margins of the ceremony, Minister of Foreign Affairs Emil Hurezeanu also had a bilateral meeting with the President of the European Parliament. The two officials emphasized the importance of continuing close cooperation between Romania and the European Parliament in order to identify and promote pragmatic joint solutions to current challenges, for the benefit of the Union and its citizens.

Referring to European priorities for the coming period, Minister Emil Hurezeanu emphasized the importance of continuing the enlargement process with both Eastern partners, such as the Republic of Moldova and Ukraine, and Western Balkan candidate countries. The Minister of Foreign Affairs emphasized the importance of cooperation with the European Parliament in promoting the Union's political objectives as set out in the Strategic Agenda 2024-2029. In this context, they mentioned the need to strengthen democratic resilience, consolidate European competitiveness, take joint action to ensure an ambitious European budget for the future and foster a strong transatlantic relationship.

The English speech delivered by the Minister of Foreign Affairs at the Timișoara Award for European Values ceremony, which was presented to the President of the European Parliament Roberta Metsola, can be found below.

***

Your Excellency President Roberta Metsola,

Mayor Dominic Fritz,

Esteemed members of the jury of the Timișoara Award for European Values ,

Ladies and gentlemen,

It is both an honour and a pleasure for me to join you today in the beautiful city of Timișoara to honour a great friend of Romania – her Excellency Roberta Metsola, the President of the European Parliament. Thank you, Mayor Dominic Fritz, for this invitation.

I particularly welcome the decision of the jury to choose Roberta Metsola as the laureate of the 2025 edition of the Timișoara Award for European Values. And which Romanian city is more suitable to host such a ceremony dedicated to the promotion and protection of European values than Timișoara? It is the beating democratic heart of Romania!

Because it was in Timișoara where the Romanian Revolution started 35 years ago, paving the way for our deep European transformation.

We are now a proud EU Member State, as we found our place in a community of 27 like-minded states that work together to promote the European ideal – that of peace, stability, and solidarity.

On a personal note, Timișoara holds a very special place in our minds and hearts. In December 1989, I was an editor of Radio Free Europe in München and I vividly recollect the dramatic moments when the flame of the Romanian revolution ignited in Timisoara. From the 17 th of December onwards, myself and my colleagues informed with emotion and hope, via radio wave, about Timișoara's fight for our freedom, for the right of Romanians to live in a free and democratic society.

Timișoara will always be a symbol of freedom and dignity, the city which placed Romania on the map of democratic states.

Those days will always be reminding us that it takes courage and sacrifice to stand by our right for democratic choices.

The attachment to the European project, which represents the very heart of President Metsola's political vision, is also our faith for the future of Romania. And we have seen that in times of uncertainty and unpredictability, when our democratic beliefs are targeted by malign actions and disinformation, we can rely on friends like President Roberta Metsola to defend and speak loudly about our common achievements in Europe.

Madam President,

You participated in many events dedicated to youth across the EU, including in Romania. You are proving that in order to build confidence in their dreams, young people need inspiring role models that can reach people with their own example of integrity, courage, kindness and genuine belief in the European way of life.

Your ability of speaking with the large public in a direct way, about Europe, with both mind and soul, represents a valuable skill in an era of disinformation, fake news, alternative facts, and polarisation, when truth and integrity become precious commodities.

Your EP Presidency is one of many firsts. You are the younger president of the European Parliament and the first lady president in more than 20 years.

You have faced major crises, which you've managed with responsibility, by always promoting the values that unite our European nations and that are making our Europe the natural choice of our partners from the East and the Western Balkans that are choosing to join the European family.

Just one month into your mandate, the Russian Federation attacked Ukraine, starting a still ongoing war with devastating effects. Your commitment has been instrumental in keeping Ukraine high on the EU agenda.

You have also been a close and dear friend of Romania, supporting our goals at EU level throughout your over a decade-long career in the European Parliament, and especially during your Presidency.

Your personal involvement in affirming time and again Romania's right to be a member of the Schengen Area contributed to a positive decision in this regard, which led to a more secure Union for all its citizens. Allow me to assure you of our high appreciation for your relentless support.

You have also been a strong supporter of the Republic of Moldova, which has advanced considerably on its European path.

The European Parliament has been backing, thanks to your personal involvement, their European choice. "Moldova's place is in Europe. Our future must be together", as you said during your visit to Chișinău in November 2022.

Madam President,

On 17 July 1979, during her inaugural speech as the first female President of the newly elected European Parliament, Simone Veil stated: "If the challenges facing Europe are to be met, we need a Europe capable of solidarity, of independence and of cooperation."

Almost 50 years later, you said in a recent speech that "there is only one path left: forward and together".

We will continue to be stronger together only as long as we will continue to stand up for each other and find common solutions to our problems in our common House – the European Union.

The Timișoara Award for European Values is a public acknowledgement of your exceptional contribution and merits to consolidating the EU and its place in the world. It is as well a token of appreciation for your continuous support for our country.

Romania's fight for a European future started here, in Timișoara, 35 years ago. Allow me to assure you that Romania is and will continue to be a country of freedom and democracy, a convergence of cultures united in diversity, just as our host city, Timișoara, is.

I congratulate you once again, Madam President, and wish you all the best.

Thank you!
